config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that can be used to justify abuse, censorship, and unfair treatment of minorities. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first, while still allowing limits when there is a clear and serious public need. As someone shaped by a country where communal values matter a lot, I still believe personal dignity and freedom should not be sacrificed too easily.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kita menunjukkan bahwa ketika “kebaikan bersama” dijadikan alasan terlalu luas, hasilnya sering berupa penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, sensor, dan penindasan terhadap kelompok kecil. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih nyaman. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang terlebih dahulu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the pull of communitarian values and social harmony, but I have also seen how easily “the public good” can be used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or excuse unfair treatment. Society is strongest when individual rights are protected first, with limits only when there is a clear, serious, and proportionate public necessity. If we reverse that order, the people with less power usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan yang berlebihan, dan sejarah kami penuh contoh ketika hak minoritas dikorbankan demi kenyamanan mayoritas. Tentu ada situasi ketika hak individu perlu dibatasi, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan dijadikan aturan default. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah menekan individu, melainkan yang paling serius melindungi martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of harmony, order, and collective responsibility, but I do not trust “society first” as a default because it can easily become a tool for silencing criticism, policing minorities, or excusing abuse by those in power. Public needs do matter, especially in crises, but any limit on individual rights should be narrow, necessary, and proportionate, not based on vague claims about the common good. In my view, a society is healthier when it protects personal dignity first and then justifies exceptions carefully.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan berlebihan, dan itu biasanya yang paling merugikan minoritas serta orang yang tidak punya banyak suara. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya dalam keadaan yang benar-benar perlu, dengan batas yang jelas dan proporsional, bukan karena mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dimulai dari perlindungan martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
